title: Discaria exilis
intro: Discaria exilis, also known as the "thorny discaria," is a small, woody shrub native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used by the Aboriginal people to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Discaria exilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and other health conditions.

It belongs to the order Rosales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae, indicating its status as a plant species.
